Open Telemark and Feather Ending
Foxtrot Open Telemark and Feather Ending
The Open Telemark and Feather Ending is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1 
Compress down through right shoulder, hip and foot. Use CBM to send lady to inside turn. Rise through LF leading heel turn. Then, let right hip swing ahead. 
Q: RF to side | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
When leading to PP ending, Man left his LF there (like in spiral turn), there is no brush, LF then steps out in step 3 to PP.
Base rotate to left more than PP
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd in PP | Pointing DW | Body Facing Wall | TH | 1/2 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Body turn less to PP. Keep body facing Lady.
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DW | Body Facing Wall | HT | -- | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 4
After stepping on RF, anchor RF, allow L leg to swing ahead of body, create diagonal stretch. 
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP | Facing DW | T | Sway(R) | Up
Upper body rotate slightly left, indicating Lady to close her head and do Feather Finish
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Left turning figures - anchor right shoulder and hip down, turn the base. keep upward leftward diagonal stretch. Do not turn L shoulder back.
Do not turn head to PP on this count, it's too early.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Do not lock the knees, keep knees flexed, keep action soft. Take stomach muscle back.
Allow arms and upper body to continue following the Man, upper body will finish the turn.
Head started turning to PP into next step.
Q: RF diag fwd in PP, R side leading | Pointing to LOD | TH | slight body turn to L | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Body turn more to PP.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to LOD (Moving DW) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
It's different to do feather finish from PP. Lady really has to use this step 4 (S) and next step 5 (Q) to move a lot of space to keep up with Man.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing Wall | TH | 1/4 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Swing R hip forward, then allow feet to turn under the body.
Body never feels travel side ways. Body feels like traveling forward.
R side stretch diagonally.
Keep arms forward in front of body.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6, body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR Lower e/o6
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Dance Central - Open Impetus
Foxtrot Open Impetus
The Open Impetus is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Maintain the sway from previous figure as you lower through CBM, keep the diagonal stretch.
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2
Do not lift the toe off the floor. The ball of the foot needs to stay skimming the floor.
Q: LF diag. fwd in PP, L side leading | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | TH | slight body turn to R | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Change sway to PP, pull across thighs, hips, rib cage, sending head forward, rotate to right to send lady to PP.
Finish with body slightly ahead of Lady.
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | HT | -- | Sway(S) | rise e/o 4
Follower
S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -- 
Keep traveling forward. Big step.
Do not think about turning head position too early. Think about body position staying closed as long as possible.
Q: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2
Do not turn too early, swing left side up (do not drop left shoulder, to be light on Man), stay behind Man.
Follow Man's hip, gauge step size, if too big, it can gap and throw Man off. Just big enough to stay behind Man.
Allow feet to turn. Pull center back.
Q: RF to side in PP having brushed to LF | Pointing Center, moving DC | TH | 3/8 btwn 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Let head finish the action at the very last. Head turn slowly.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing Center, moving DC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 4

Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel and Feather Ending
Foxtrot Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel and Feather Ending
The Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel and Feather Ending is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | HT | start to turn L | S | CBM | rise e/o 1
Compress right shoulder, hip and foot, stretch through diagonal, rise up through left foot leading heel turn.
Leave LF here, it becomes like spiral a little bit (when leading into Telemark.)
Q: RF to side | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
After swinging RF to side, swivel feet (base area) more than upper body. Upper body belongs to Lady.
Q: LF sideways in PP | Facing DW against LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
This completes the "Open Telemark" part of the figure. "Natural Turn" part only applies to Leader. Lady's steps are not Lady's Natural Turn steps.
S: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DW against LOD (Moving to Wall) | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 4
RF fwd in lane 1. Lady is in lane 2.
Q: LF to side | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | Up
Man is making lane change to the other side of the Lady (lane 3). Do not bring Lady across you, Man makes lane change.
Stretch left side.
Q: RF back R side leading | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6 
Compress right side, leading Lady into swivel action. Rotate through the hip, arms keep rotating across the body to right (arms are still in front of body, not behind.)
S: LF back in CBMP, RF crosses in front without weight. End in PP | DW Toe turned in. End facing DC | TH with pressure on T of RF | 1/4 turn to R on 7 | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Keep head poised through diagonal. Diagonal shape is important, do not take head weight back.
S: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DC (moving to center) | HT | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 8
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DC | T | Sway(R) | Up
Use the leverage of R leg, swing left leg fwd ahead of body, closing the lady. Do not rotate Lady too far in upper body. Still keep an angle to come through for feather ending.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 10
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM
Note -- When the RF starts to move to cross in front on step 7, either the Heel or Toe may be in contact with the floor. It will finish with the Toe in contact with the floor and the Heel only slightly raised with the R leg almost straight. 
The Weave from PP could follow step 7 in which case the aligment of the Weave from PP would be as in the Waltz Weave from PP. 
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could be danced by checking OP on any Feather Movement taken DC and turning 1/4 to R. Follow --Silver--Feather Ending--Natural Turn (when danced as steps 4-6 above)--Natural Weave from PP--Gold--Curved Feather to Back Feather from PP--Natural Zig-Zag from PP
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Don't open head too early here, wait till end of next step Q.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | Facing DW | HT | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Heel Turn is an abrupt, steep turn for Lady. 
When turning left, the right side is the anchor point, left side stretch. 
First part of turn is done through feet (base), feet turn more. Do not bring shoulders back too quickly.
Head is still L. Head slowly turns between end of this step and next Q. Don't open head too early, arrive on L leg first, wait for Man's lead to open head. Turn head slowly "milk it".
Q: RF diag fwd in PP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Second part is to finish the turn with upper body. 
Imagine always dancing forward.
Head turns to Promenade. 
This completes the "Open Telemark" part of the figure. "Natural Turn" part only applies to Leader. Lady's steps are not Lady's Natural Turn steps.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DW (moving to wall) | HT | -- | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 4
Very important for lady to finish behind Man on previous step. Then Lady's L leg should be behind Man's R leg and and should track Man's R leg movement forward, so it won't feel like Man is blocking Lady.
Allow Man to get ahead of you. 
Left shoulder connects to left hip. Keep shaping to left.
Allow arms to swing across the body.
Then dance straight forward, follow the Man with upper body, legs are tracking straight.
Q: RF fwd, between partner's feet | Facing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | Up. 
Keep the shaping to left, allow R side body/shoulder go forward to Man. Swing R hip forward. Use standing leg to push, don't fall forward. 
Head position changes to left. Leave head behind, send middle of body forward, finish head with more extension. Keeping the wound-up shape for next step.
Movement is smooth and continuous, no stopping. 
One style is to keep slight R side lead through the Passing Natural. 
Q: LF fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6
LF straight fwd. Relax into L knee/leg, head shaping leftward back, wind up for next swivel.
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP. LF closes slightly back, without weight. End in PP | Facing DW. End facing DC against LOD, (sometimes more turn is done, ending traveling DC) | HTH and pressure on IE of T of LF | 1/2 to R on 7 | Sway(S) | CBM | --
This is the swivel. Bigger step, keep shoulder parallel, turn hips and head. The swivel action is purely happening underneath the body, do not turn the shoulders.
Shoulder should end parallel with Man with hips turned out more.
Hips and and feet are in same alignment.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DC against LOD (moving to center) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 8
Keep behind Man's hip.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 8-9 | Sway(L) | Up
Swing right side up and forward. Head back to left.
Feather finish through the base.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 9-10, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 10
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Can follow with Basic Weave.

Dance Central - Weave From PP
Foxtrot Weave from Promenade Position
The Weave from Promenade Position is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC Body facing LOD | HT | Sway(S) | rise e/o 1
feet, low center, high center: the offset position of these 3 points must be maintained through PP and weave. 
Q: LF fwd | Facing DC | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up
Angle left shoulder and hip down, do not pull weight back.
Step through a curved line.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Energy still moves diagonal center. Do not think about BLOD.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(L) | Up
Pull center back
Q: RF back | Backing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Connect R shoulder and hip down, Man is inside of turn.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 between 5-6 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 7
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | Sway(S) | CBM
Note -- On step 4 the Footwork of TH may be used. The Rise on 5 will then be "Up NFR".
Alternate alignments for the Weave from PP may be found in the Waltz charts.
Follower
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to center (moving DC) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Keep center toward the Man.
Body doesn't turn till second step.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DC | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
Depends on how Man shapes. Head can keep left or right. Head following the arc.
If shaping to R, keep head position, turn body underneath (base rotate), effectively, head will shape to right during weave. 
Think step forward and it will end in "side and slightly back"
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 2-3 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Keep knee soft, do not stick tummy to man, pull center back.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Q : LF fwd | Facing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up. 
R side is the driving side, left side is swinging fwd. The driving side is always the lower side, not the swinging side.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/4 between 5-6 | Sway(L) | Up
Closing head to left.
Swivel the feet. Feet turns more.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 6-7 Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 7
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Dance Central - Top Spin
Foxtrot Top Spin
The Top Spin is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
Preceding step is generally a Feather Finish. To continue with Top Spin (to avoid traffic for example), the previous Feather Finish is cut short so you can respond quickly into Top Spin action. 
"Backing Against LOD" means Man is facing LOD but your next step will be stepping backward (against LOD). This is different from BLOD, which is "Backing LOD" your next step is down LOD.
Start turning left hip to left. This is not a large step. Then use CBM. 
Q: RF back | Backing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | UP
Connect right shoulder down to right side.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DC of new LOD | T | 1/4 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(R) | UP
Swing left hip forward.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Lower e/o 4
See Note for other alignments. Could finish DW of new LOD.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Q: RF fwd in CBMP OP | Facing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
When Man cut short Feather Finish, Lady respond to keep weight on T (at end of Feather Finish).
Follow Man's body, do not be too powerful, the figure is compact. Mainly used in corners or get out of trouble. Man can change alignments. 
"Facing Against LOD": think of typical Feather Finish ending, you'll be changing direction, so you'll be stepping forward (against LOD).
Q: LF fwd | Facing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Monitor through Man's central line, keep arm light.
Connect head, shoulder hip to left foot.
Q: RF to side | Backing new LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Allow feet to swivel, feet turns more than body
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 3-4, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 4
Think about stretching diagonally to the right, rather than rotation in upper body.
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Notes
On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. 
On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW.
Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW.
Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

Curved Feather to Back Feather
Foxtrot Curved Feather to Back Feather
The Foxtrot Curved Feather to Back Feather is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing DW | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing wall | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | R | CBM | Up. Lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1.8 between 3-4 | S | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4, NFR
Gradual rise is used in Back Feather.
Q: RF back R side leading | Curving toward LOD | T | - | Sway (L) | | Continue to rise on 5
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway (L) | | Up. Lower e/o 6.
S: RF back | Backing LOD | T | - | Sway (S) | CBM | - | 
Follower
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR
Curve, the rotation is mostly in the hip area, follow Man's hip, don't stop turning to R (other you'll block Man), continue the rotation and give Man middle and side and stay connected. 
Upper body/shoulder should not turn too much which will pull off from partner (but it does turn to R, otherwise Man can't pass). Upper body is diagonally stretched (R side is upward), keep parallel to partner.
Push off standing leg to move. 
Forward with body and arms.
Q: RF back, R side leading | Backing DW | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Follow leader's direction - there maybe feeling of a forward step, then body turns.
Hip turns more with Man, body turns less (wind up, shoulder should not be flat with hip), body prepares to swing for next step. Shoulder keep parallel with Man. 
Lady can lower her R heel on step 2.
Head to L.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing Wall | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | CBM | Up. Lower e/o 3
Small step LF behind RF, shape to L, like winding up to go. Lady should lower late on 3.
Hip and body rotating to R, follow Man's hips, rotate hip to Man, connected with Man, hip is still in front of Man, not to the side of Man's hip.
Leave RF loosely in front, do not stick R leg too early.
Prepare to really use LF to push for next RF fwd step. This is the circling movement action, get weight into heel and then push forward.
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | HT | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4
Rotate hip more and lower more into L knee, push off LF/L leg to drive for bigger movement. 
Do no t going downward in this step. The lowering happens in previous step (weight into heel on previous Q). This steps drives forward and up (rise e/o this step).
Left side now swings forward (body does not stop - which will make it look like going backward), do not swing too much up, focus on forward, body rotate a lot to the right. 
Head turns and shapes to R. Do not cross into Man's space. Smooth changes. 
Movement should feel "free", not constrained. Body breathes.
Q: LF fwd L side leading | Curving towards LOD | T | - | Sway(R) | Continue to rise on 5
If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R, but stay very left, keep ribs/side to Man.
Really needs to turn body (rotate to R)
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(R) | Up, Lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | H | - | S | CBM | - 
Typically, continue with Feather Finish SQQ. 
This first LF fwd on S, do not follow Man's curve, step straight forward in between Man's legs, otherwise, at the end of Feather finish, Lady's hip will gap/end up to the side of Man's hip. 
When swing on Q: do not dip shoulder low, keep should flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. 
Breathe, release so body does not stiff up.
if Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R on QQ, keep ribs to Man.

Dance Central - Natural Twist Turn
The Foxtrot Natural Twist Turn is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Natural Twist Turn with Hover Feather Ending
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Continue to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps.
Q (1/2): LF to side | Backing DC | TH | 3/8 between steps 1-2 | Sway(R) | -
& (1/2): RF crosses behind LF slightly back | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | -
Q: Twist on both feet. | Facing DC | Twist on T of RF and H of LF with feet flat. End with weight on RF | 3/8 on steps 4-5. Body turns slightly more. | Sway(S) 
S: End as in Heel Pull | Facing DC | T of RF with pressure on IE of T of LF | Sway(L) | Rise on 5. Body overturns on step 5.
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DC | T | - | Sway(L) 
Complete figure with a Hover Feather
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | - | Sway(S) 
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM 
Follower
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | Sway(S) | CBM | Continue to turn R
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps.
Head can keep left during the whole figure. Don't go back to straight position. Keep very left, more extended back during Weave.
Connect L shoulder down, Feel partner on the right side.
Styling option: start with head to L and by end of Q&Q, head shape to R.
Q (1/2): RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Backing LOD | HT | Sway(L) | 1/2 between 1-2
Heel turn. Positive R side stretch.

Curve to left, make sure to still give left side forward to Man, keep head very left, really extend. Don't fall back.
Continue moving center with Man and leave head behind to create the shape.
Q: RF side | PLOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | body turns less | Sway(L) | Up
Make sure to get out of Man's way, RF moving direction is not down LOD, it's BDW, foot is pointing LOD. 
Follow Man's hip to gauge RF step size. If too big, Lady will get in front of Man. Lady is inside of the turn.

Not as much rise, knee stay flexed, There is Sway and Shape to L, but much faster. Lower into legs/knees faster. "drop faster". Use the power on movement. 
S: RF fwd (pivot) | FLOD | HTH | (Heel is kept in contact with floor) | up to 1/2 to R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Heel is in contact with floor, though footwork is HTH.
Keep upper thigh contact with partner (R to R leg) for all pivot and spin turns.
Keep strong elbow, stay to left, elbow turn, leg just follow elbow.
Stay low, don't rise.
LF is not held in CBMP for lady, but CBM will be used on next step except when Natural Spin Turn follows the pivot.
Keep feet on two tracks at end of this step.
After pivot, posture should be still good, upper body should not be forward. R hip forward, don't stick butt out. It needs to finish with full weight on RF before pushing RF to drive to next step. Don't fall into next step.
There is a tendency for body to lean forward and move to R. (Try not turning head to R. Turning head to R tends to make body shift to R, which is not good.) Stay very left (Man's R side).
Shaping: often this shapes to R. Head to R.
Notes:
Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn.
At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD.
Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW.
When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2
step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 3/8
step 3-4: lady: Lady: 1/8
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8
step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4
step 3-4: Lady 1/8

QuickStep Running Right Turn

Think of Running Right Turn as a couple of figures strung together: Natural Pivot, Half Natural Turn (Heel Turn for Lady), Running Finish (OP)
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Step straight forward, do not veer to right, 
Q: LF side | BDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | up

Settle at end.

(Pivot): do not lean forward and stick butt out.
S: RF fwd in CBMP | facing LOD | HT |continue to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 5
Man step straight fwd
S: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 btwn 5-6 | Sway(R) | up
Man does not have to complete turn with his body on this step, the turn completes through next step

Body completes turn

Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Don't be "steppy" - all steps are smooth flowing.
Slightly to side to give Man room to step. Use CBM to open door for Man to pass.
Q: RF to side | Pointing LOD | T | 3/8 to R between 1-2, body turns less | Sway(L) | up
R leg track Man's R leg. Otherwise Lady will gap with Man.
Extend head to L.
Q: LF closes to RF | Facing LOD | TH | body completes turn | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
Feet together, settle and prep legs. Do not break at hip or stick butt out. 
S: RF fwd | LOD | HTH | 1/2 to R on 4 (pivot) | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Pivot on RF.
Lady can keep head strongly to the left; be careful not to bring head into Man's space.
Or, Lady can turn her head to right (which, when done right is preferable, will help Man with the pivot.). 
Head should stay on Man's right side, do not let head get in front of Man if turning head to right.
When shaping head to right, it's both a slight "dip" to right, as well as rotating to the right at the same time.
Keep L leg where it is, do not close up with R leg after pivot, need to leave room for Man. Man is tracking the leg.
During the pivot and heel turn, keep right side "positive" (in front of body, stretched)
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | continue to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 5, NFR
L leg should have been been left where it was during pivot, just step back.
Curve.
S: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | facing LOD | HT | 1/2 btwn 5-6 | Sway(L) | up
Heel turn. Draw in R heel slowly, R side is slightly raised for a quick moment. (Man lifting side for heel turn.) Do not close both feet too fast.
Leave head, head stretched very left, send middle forward to Man, turn body, so head is stretched to left even more. hip more forward. which makes head extension bigger. Then stay connected with Man. 
S: LF fwd preparing to step OP, L side leading | facing LOD | TH | - | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 7
At beginning of this step, R side is still positive till getting ready for next step (RF OP).
Rise on toes and sending middle part of body and left side strongly forward and around Man (to keep connection with Man). LF forward, push from standing R leg. Keep body rotating, very twisted to R, head stretched. 
It appears to have big extension, when in fact it's just sending middle body forward. L toe keep pressure on floor to keep balance.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | facing LOD | HT | starts to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 8
This timing is QQS. Running Finish is also often danced as SQQ.
SQQ: Running Finish, head turned to R. Don't get too close to Man, body to Man, but extend to R. Ending weight on RF, get into R knee.
RF strong forward step. Keep left side up and around Man. Do not break at the hip. Make sure the whole R side including middle of body is still connected with Man, then stretch as much as possible.
Styling option: head turn to right, but body still needs to stay to left, only turn head
Q: LF to side | BDC of new LOD | T | 1/8 btwn 8-9 | Sway(R) | up
Point LF forward, big step, swing L leg out ahead of body, then swivel LF (it becomes a side step). 
Styling option: turn head to R (if shaping to R, commit to it, don't do it half way.)
S: RF back, R side leading | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 9-10 | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 10
Ending weight on RF, get into R knee. Don't fall back to next step.
Keep head distance from Man, don't close up head space with Man, keep frontal curve back. Stay left.
At end of the step, R side is positive.
S: LF back in CBMP | BLOD | T | - | Sway(S) | CBM | --
e.g followed by a Natural Turn. If head had turned to right earlier in the Running Finish, keep head there, turn body to right, which will return head to normal left position. Keep it smooth.
Notes:
Other alignments:
Across corner: 3/8 on Pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish.
Two corners: 3/8 on Pivot, 3/8 on Natural Turn, 1/4 on Running Finish
Side of room: 1/2 on pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish
When 3/8 is made on Running Finish, using "pointing" instead of "facing" at step 9 for Man.
Alternative timing SQQ may be used on steps 5-7, in which case, SQQS will be used on the Running Finish.

Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

Quickstep Progressive Chassé To Right
The Quickstep Progressive Chasse To Right is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | HT | Start to turn L | -- | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | T | 1/8 between 1 and 2 | -- | continue to rise on 2 and 3
Q: LF closes to RF | Backing DW | T | 1/8 between 2 and 3, body turns less | -- | Up, lower e/o 4
S: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | TH | -- | -- | --
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | T | -- | -- | CBM 
Next step would be in Outside Partner position.
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DC | TH | Begin to turn L | - | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Q: LF to side | Pointing DW | T | 1/4 between 1 and 2, body turns less | - | continue to rise on 2 and 3
Q: RF closes to LF | Facing DW | T | slight body turn | - | --
L side is strongly projected, but do not fall to left side too quickly which will pull Man.
S: LF to side and slightly fwd | Facing DW | TH | -- | - | --
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | H | -- | - | CBM
Notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is mad the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

Dance Central - Double Reverse Spin
Quickstep Double Reverse Spin
The Quickstep Double Reverse Spin is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
The technical details are the same as in Waltz Double Reverse Spin.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Stars to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise at end of 1
Do not collapse left side. Stretch left side and not lean toward inside of the turn.
Rise to toe - early rise.
S: RF to side | BDW | T | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
Wait until the lady's feet are together before taking this step.
S: LF closes to RF without weight (Toe Pivot) | Facing DW | T | 1/2 btwn 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up, Lower at end of 3
Follower
S: RF back | BLOD | TH | starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Very important to do CBM, with CBM shoulder will clear way for Man to pass. keep shoulder flat, and do not drop it on one side.
Use head weight back.
RF bigger step back.
Make sure keep body and head very left to Man, keep left side lifted. Keep the volume of the frame. Do no allow shoulder to rotate too quickly pass the hips, which will pull the Man into Lady's space and making hips rotate away from partner.
Do not take elbow back. Think about giving the arm to Man, keeping it forward throughout the figure. Take R shoulder onto RF, not pass RF.
S: LF closes to RF (Heel turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 1/2 between 1 and 2 | Sway(S) | Cont to rise on 2
Draws trailing foot in slowly, both legs should straighten,
Turn head slightly more to left (don't do it too early, though)
Draw center back to spine. Do not leave body to Man.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing wall | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Bigger step fwd, stay with Man, keep on his R side. Slight swing.
Make this fluid with previous heel turn and use LF to push off, then swivel on RF. Like in Viennese Waltz.
Keeping arms forward, give arms to Man.
Q: LF crosses in front of RF | BDW | TH | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Do not lower too early, lower on "&"
Finish with L side positive/fwd.
Note:
The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW.
When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2
step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 3/8
step 3-4: lady: Lady: 1/8
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8
step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4
step 3-4: Lady 1/8

Dance Central - Cross Chasse
Quickstep Cross Chasse
The Quickstep Cross Chasse is a Bronze Level figure, listed as an additional step that can be danced in Medal Tests. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd, OP | FDW | H 
Man steps OP.
S: LF fwd | FDW | HT | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF closes to RF | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
At the end of this, the body is wound up perfect for leading natural turning figures (with first step CBMP , OP)
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Continue into Forward Lock in Outside Partner Position. Use CBM as you take the step.
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH 
Lengthen R side.
S: RF back | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR
Lady connects R should down.
Q: LF to side | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Maintain the shape, push on RF/thigh. 
Styling option: turn head to R
Q: RF closes to LF | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Running Cross Chasse
In stead of closing feet above, keep moving ('running") forward:
Leader
S: RF fwd, CBMP, OP | FDW | HT 
S: LF fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF diagonally fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF fwd, L shoulder leading | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH 
Pay attention not to do a lot rotation through shoulders.
S: RF back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR 
R shoulder connects down. 
Q: LF diagonally back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Do not rotate L shoulder.
Stretch L side.
Q: RF back, R shoulder leading | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
